236.reason: [Error: No bounds check before accessing rawCode.charAt(pos) after incrementing pos. If pos equals rawCode.length(), this will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException, causing the program to crash.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: No bounds check before accessing rawCode.charAt(pos) after incrementing pos. If pos equals rawCode.length(), this will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException, causing the program to crash.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Missing boundary check: pos may exceed rawCode.length() causing StringIndexOutOfBoundsException
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [If pos reaches the end of rawCode, charAt(pos) will throw StringIndexOutOfBoundsException]

253.reason: [When encountering a single '|' or '&', the code correctly detects error 'a', but it does not skip the invalid character. The lexer continues processing and may re-read the same invalid character in the next iteration, potentially causing an infinite loop or duplicate error reporting. More critically, after reporting the error, the invalid token is not consumed, and since `type` remains null, nothing is added to the token list—but the character has already been read and will be processed again. However, the real issue is that the error is reported but the lexer doesn't advance past the invalid single '&' or '|', leading to repeated processing of the same character. But even more importantly: the specification says each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error, and the program must output only the error. However, in the current logic, after adding the error, the loop continues and may process the same character again because `pos` was decremented. This can cause the same error to be reported multiple times or lead to unexpected behavior. However, the immediate bug is that when a single '&' or '|' is encountered, the code reports an error but then leaves the character to be reprocessed—yet since no token is emitted and the character is not whitespace, it will be encountered again in the next loop iteration, causing an infinite loop. For example, input "1 & 2" will read '&', increment pos, see next char is space (not '&'), decrement pos back to '&', report error, then next loop iteration sees '&' again—repeat forever.]
